name: United States
headline: United States CARRY-FORWARD CONFIRMATION at 25.0 / Developing — Rotation backfill cycle (18-day staleness since April 29 confirmation). May 17 sector-context signals include tech-layoff wave (Meta May 20 8,000-person layoff pre-execution window; Oracle May 8 severance-rejection coverage; Microsoft/Amazon/Google AI-pivot displacement pattern) and ongoing immigration enforcement trajectory. Government-level conduct: federal regulatory posture sustained; state-level differential treatment patterns (Texas SB4 immigration, Florida education restrictions, California climate leadership) continue under federalism framework. No new May 17 entity-specific federal-governance event warrants score change. Sector signals (tech layoffs) are scored against individual corporate entities (Meta, Oracle, etc.), not against the federal-government entity, per parent-vs-corporate-entity scoping methodology. Confirm at 25.0 / Developing.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Federal awareness infrastructure operational (CDC, BLS, federal statistics agencies); state-level recognition heterogeneous.

EMP.rationale: Sustained. Federal safety-net programs (Medicaid, SNAP, Social Security) operational; state-level EMP application heterogeneous.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. AC2 proportionality: federal capacity-action gap on documented issues (housing, healthcare access, mental health); AC4 resource mobilization functional but selectively applied.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. Racial wealth gap, criminal-justice differential treatment, immigration enforcement differential patterns continue; EQU-dimension structurally constrained by federalism.

BND.rationale: Sustained. Constitutional-rights framework operational; IHRL framework engagement partial (treaty ratification gaps); IHL framework engagement selective.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. Independent judicial accountability operational (federal courts, civil rights enforcement); FOIA infrastructure functional; corporate accountability through SEC/DOJ/FTC functioning.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. Systems-of-care operational at federal-state-local levels with heterogeneous quality; healthcare-access gap structural.

INT.rationale: Sustained. Federal governance posture under pressure shows institutional persistence; INT-dimension consistency framework partially met across democratic transitions.

evidence.summary: April 29 confirmation cycle established current baseline at 25.0 / Developing. Rotation backfill (18-day staleness) revisits.

Tech-layoff wave (Meta May 20 8,000; Oracle May 8 severance; Microsoft/Amazon/Google AI-pivot) scored against individual corporate entities per parent-vs-corporate-entity-scoping methodology, not against federal-government entity.
Federal regulatory posture sustained; no acute May 17 entity-specific federal-governance event (legislation, executive action, judicial ruling) warranting score change.

State-level differential treatment patterns (Texas SB4 immigration, Florida education restrictions, California climate leadership) continue under federalism framework; aggregated into federal-entity score with state-level heterogeneity recognized.

keyEvidence: Rotation backfill cycle (18-day staleness); baseline 25.0 / Developing confirmed.
Tech-layoff sector signals scored against corporate entities, not federal entity.
No new May 17 entity-specific federal-governance event.
State-level heterogeneity aggregated; federalism framework recognized.
rationale: Confirm at 25.0 / Developing. The April 29 baseline is sustained; no new May 17 entity-specific federal-governance event warrants score change. Sector signals (tech layoffs) are appropriately scored against individual corporate entities (Meta Platforms, Oracle) under parent-vs-corporate-entity-scoping methodology, not against the federal-government entity. State-level differential treatment patterns are aggregated into the federal score under federalism framework with heterogeneity recognized. Math: (2+2+2+1.8+2.3+2+2+2)/8 = 16.1/8 = 2.0125 → ((2.0125-1)/4)*100 = 25.3125 → 25.3. Note: published composite is stated as 25.0; reconstructed math yields 25.3. Math-hygiene flag for dimensional-vs-composite drift (0.3pt gap); documented for downstream reconciliation.
watches: Federal legislative action on immigration, healthcare, climate
Judicial rulings affecting EQU-dimension (Supreme Court decisions)
State-federal preemption disputes (Texas SB4, climate regulations)
Tech-sector labor protections (potential federal response to AI-pivot displacement)
International commitments (Paris Agreement, IHL treaty engagement)
carryForward: Federalism framework state-level heterogeneity aggregation principle
Independent judicial accountability ACC-dimension positive anchor
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Composite 25.0 sits 5.0pt above Critical/Developing (20/21) and 16.0pt below Developing/Functional (40/41).

math_check.validator_check: Math drift flag: dimensional-sum-derived 25.3 vs published 25.0 (0.3pt gap). Carry-forward confirmation; no proposed change. Math-hygiene reconciliation flag documented.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
